signals.title: USD/CHF — Geopolitical Shock + Carry Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF trades at 0.8118 following reports that Iran struck US military facilities in Jordan and Bahrain. While the 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage structurally supports USD/CHF, the acute geopolitical escalation triggers immediate safe-haven demand for the Franc. The pair is currently holding above its SMA20/50/200 cluster (0.8080/0.7982/0.7918), which serves as the primary technical floor. SNB's 0.00% policy rate and -0.62% real rate maintain CHF as the premier funding currency, but safe-haven flows typically override carry logic during active military engagements. VIX at 17.5 suggests cross-asset stress is not yet at a full 'risk-off' regime (VIX > 25), meaning the CHF bid may remain localized to geopolitical headlines. Risk: De-escalation headlines trigger a rapid snap-back toward 0.8200 as the 375bp carry advantage reasserts dominance.
evidence: Headline: Iran struck US military facilities

signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD trades at 1.3373, pinned near its SMA50 (1.3375) as the British Pound emerges as the G10 laggard this week. The G10 scorecard shows a 0.879% spread between the leading USD and lagging GBP, driven by a moderate negative CESI bias for the UK (-5 net misses) vs a strong positive bias for the US (+47 net beats). COT data shows speculators remain net long GBP (+28,278), creating a positioning overhang as the currency underperforms its peers. The 0bp OIS carry differential (SOFR 3.57% vs SONIA 3.73%) provides no yield cushion for the Pound against the Greenback's data-backed momentum. A sustained break below the SMA20/50 cluster at 1.3351/1.3375 would signal a deeper correction toward the SMA200 at 1.3401. Risk: BoE meeting on 30 July delivers a hawkish surprise that forces a re-accumulation of GBP longs.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Policy Shift + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY is testing 162.97, holding above its SMA20 (162.13) despite intensifying verbal intervention and hawkish BoJ signaling. Bloomberg reports the BoJ is open to a faster rate hike pace as Yen weakness adds to price risk, directly challenging the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. The options market is pricing significant downside protection, with the 25d Risk Reversal at -1.42 (moderate put skew) and a 3.6-point Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.6%). Retail sentiment is at a 84% short extreme, diverging from the COT LF net short of -105,238, suggesting a highly crowded trade vulnerable to a policy-induced squeeze. A break below the SMA20 at 162.13 would confirm the options market's bearish bias and target the 160.00 handle. Risk: BoJ holds steady on 31 July, allowing the 275bp carry to. toward 165.00.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Geopolitics
signals.text: NZD/USD trades at 0.5819, struggling to maintain its position above the SMA200 (0.5823) as Middle East tensions offset RBNZ hawkishness. COT data shows a heavy speculative net short of -29,582 (15.2% long), which directly contradicts the RBNZ's hiking stance and -0.58% accommodative real rate. RBNZ holds at 2.5%. The CESI for NZD is neutral (+1), providing little fundamental support to counter the broader 'flight to safety' into the USD. If geopolitical tensions ease, the crowded spec short is at risk of a squeeze back toward 0.5900. Risk: RBNZ signals a pause in the hiking cycle, validating the existing speculative short position.
